title: HUF Universal Knob Map — Standard Control Surface for HUF-GOV Spectrum Analyzer
contributed_by: Gemini (initial knob map concept), ChatGPT (metric correction that enabled dual-trace)
triggered_by: Peter: 'can a simple huf control knob naming be used... make huf the standard to learn so one instrument does all the work'

design_principle.statement: One instrument, one language, every domain. The operator learns five readings. The domain provides one configuration. The math never changes.
design_principle.analogy: An FFT spectrum analyzer uses the same labels — Frequency, Amplitude, Phase — whether the DUT is a loudspeaker, a radio, or a bridge vibration sensor. The operator learns the analyzer once. Each domain provides a setup guide for connecting the DUT. HUF works the same way.
design_principle.conference_test: If Peter says 'Velocity' at a conference, nobody should yell 'you mean the TV distance' or 'oh the L1 norm.' Velocity IS the standard name. The math lives in the manual. The name lives on the panel.

the_five_knobs.note: These are the HUF-GOV standard panel readings. GOV-only — no control knobs. This is a measurement instrument, not a controller.

1_SOURCE.what_it_is: The raw proportional input being monitored. The carrier.
1_SOURCE.mathematical_identity: Ratio-state vector ρ = (ρ₁, ρ₂, ..., ρₙ) where each element is a proportion of the whole.
1_SOURCE.operator_reads: What am I looking at? The list of elements that make up the system.

examples.electricity: Coal, Gas, Nuclear, Hydro, Solar, Wind, Bioenergy, Other Fossil, Other Renewables
examples.finance: Technology, Healthcare, Financials, Consumer Disc., Consumer Staples, Industrials, Energy, Utilities, Materials
examples.wetlands: Species A hectares, Species B hectares, ..., Open water, Bare ground
examples.datacenter: Drive Model A count, Drive Model B count, ...

1_SOURCE.configuration: Set by App Note. The only thing that changes between domains.
1_SOURCE.panel_display: Stacked bar or pie — shows the current proportional state of the carrier

2_CALIBRATION.what_it_is: Does the Source sum to one? If not, the instrument is misconfigured or the data is malformed.
2_CALIBRATION.mathematical_identity: Σρᵢ = 1 (unity sum constraint). This is the simplex condition.
2_CALIBRATION.operator_reads: A green checkmark or a red warning. Binary. Either the input is valid or it isn't.
2_CALIBRATION.analogy: Zeroing SMAART before a measurement. If calibration fails, nothing downstream is trustworthy.
2_CALIBRATION.panel_display: Single indicator light — green (calibrated) or red (check source data)

3_COMPLEXITY.what_it_is: How evenly distributed is the Source across its elements? High = diverse and balanced. Low = concentrated in few elements.
3_COMPLEXITY.mathematical_identity: K_eff = exp(H) where H = -Σ pᵢ ln(pᵢ) is Shannon entropy. Range: 1 (total concentration in one element) to n (perfectly uniform across n elements).
3_COMPLEXITY.operator_reads: The main trace. When this drops while Velocity stays flat, something is quietly concentrating. That's the pattern.
3_COMPLEXITY.analogy: Spectral width in audio. A narrow spectrum means energy is concentrated in few frequencies. A wide spectrum means energy is distributed. Complexity is the spectral width of the carrier.
3_COMPLEXITY.panel_display: Primary trace line (blue). Time series. The most important reading on the instrument.
3_COMPLEXITY.critical_pattern: Complexity falling + Velocity calm = DECEPTIVE DRIFT. The system is homogenizing behind apparent stability.

4_VELOCITY.what_it_is: How much did the Source change since the last observation? Measures total structural movement across all elements equally.
4_VELOCITY.mathematical_identity: TV distance = 0.5 × Σ|ρᵢ(t) - ρᵢ(t-1)|. Total variation distance — the probabilistic distance between consecutive carrier states.
4_VELOCITY.operator_reads: The RMS meter. Steady and low means the surface looks calm. A spike means something moved visibly.
4_VELOCITY.analogy: RMS level meter in audio. Gives you the average energy of the change signal. Treats all elements equally — doesn't amplify outliers.
4_VELOCITY.panel_display: Secondary trace line (purple). Time series alongside Complexity.
4_VELOCITY.why_not_L2: Velocity is the primary structural velocity reading because it treats all proportional changes equally and has a natural range [0,1] for probability distributions. L2 (Peak) is the complementary reading for narrowband events.

5_PEAK.what_it_is: Was the structural change dominated by one element moving a lot, or distributed across many elements moving a little?
5_PEAK.mathematical_identity: L2 drift = √(Σ(ρᵢ(t) - ρᵢ(t-1))²). Euclidean distance — amplifies large single-component changes quadratically.
5_PEAK.operator_reads: The peak meter. When Peak is high but Velocity is moderate, one specific element is driving the change. When both are high, the whole system is moving.
5_PEAK.analogy: Peak level meter in audio. Catches transients that the RMS meter averages out. If Peak >> Velocity, you have a narrowband event. If Peak ≈ Velocity, you have broadband movement.
5_PEAK.panel_display: Secondary trace line (orange). Time series alongside Velocity.

diagnostic_use.peak_high_velocity_low: One element dominated the change — investigate which one
diagnostic_use.peak_high_velocity_high: Large broadband structural shift — the whole system moved
diagnostic_use.peak_low_velocity_high: Mathematically unlikely (L2 ≤ √2 × TVD) — check data quality

what_is_NOT_on_the_panel.note: These exist in HUF but are NOT part of the GOV spectrum analyzer. They belong to HUF-CLS (the controller) or to the underlying theory manual.

excluded.name: Sensitivity / Gain (CL-04)
excluded.reason: Control parameter. Adjusts intervention thresholds. Not observation.

excluded.name: Sync / Lock (CL-05)
excluded.reason: Closed-loop tracking state. Requires a controller to be running. Not observation.

excluded.name: S-curve / Discriminator (CL-03)
excluded.reason: Detection threshold function. Lives in the theory, not on the panel. The panel shows the RESULT (drift detected / clear), not the mechanism.

excluded.name: Sigmoid gain / Operator dynamics
excluded.reason: CLS-only. Amplifier chain. No place on a measurement instrument.

excluded.name: λ_φ
excluded.reason: Fabricated constant from Grok. Does not exist in any computed HUF document.

what_is_NOT_on_the_panel.boundary_rule: If the operator cannot observe it without closing a loop, it does not go on the GOV panel. The GOV panel is a measurement instrument. It reads. It does not adjust.

panel_status_indicators.note: Derived from the five knobs. These are the instrument's computed alerts, not additional controls.

STABLE.condition: Complexity steady or rising, Velocity within normal range
STABLE.operator_action: None required. System is diverse and stable.

DECLINING.condition: Complexity dropping but Velocity is also elevated
DECLINING.operator_action: Watch. The system is changing and the change is visible. This is normal structural evolution, not deceptive drift.

DECEPTIVE_DRIFT.condition: Complexity falling while Velocity stays calm (below median)
DECEPTIVE_DRIFT.operator_action: Investigate. The system is quietly concentrating behind apparent stability. This is the forward signal pattern.
DECEPTIVE_DRIFT.what_it_means: Something is homogenizing internally. The surface looks fine. The carrier says otherwise.

VELOCITY_SPIKE.operator_action: The structural event has arrived. Check Peak vs Velocity to determine if it's narrowband (one element) or broadband (whole system).

app_note_structure.purpose: Each domain gets one App Note that maps the domain's language to the five standard knobs. The App Note does NOT rename the knobs — it explains what the Source elements are and what observation cadence to use.

template.source_elements: What are the carrier elements? List them.
template.source_units: What are they measured in? (TWh, hectares, market cap, drive count, ...)
template.observation_cadence: How often is data available? (Daily, monthly, seasonal, annual)
template.known_structural_events: What shocks has this domain experienced? (For validation)
template.baseline_complexity: What is the typical K_eff for this domain? (For calibration)
template.domain_vocabulary_map: What does this domain already call these things? (For translation, NOT for relabeling the panel)

versioning_note.v1.0: Five GOV-only knobs. Names may be refined as more domains are computed and operator feedback is collected. The principle is fixed: one instrument, one language. The names can evolve if a better universal term emerges, but changes must be applied everywhere simultaneously — no domain gets to rename a knob locally.
versioning_note.governance: Any proposed name change must pass the conference test: if Peter says the new name on stage and nobody from any domain yells a correction, it's good.

related: METRIC-001 (L2 vs TVD — why both are on the panel)
corpus_trail.gemini_contribution: Initial 7-knob map (2 excluded as CLS). App Note structure. Peak vs RMS analogy. Spectral thinning language.
corpus_trail.chatgpt_contribution: Metric correction that created the dual-trace requirement. Falsifiability test that confirmed metric invariance.
